section_name: Employer responsibility for violations pertaining to railroad-highway grade crossings
text:
(1) An employer may not knowingly allow, require, permit, or authorize a driver to operate a commercial motor vehicle in violation of a federal, state, or local law or rule pertaining to railroad-highway grade crossings.

(2) A person who violates subsection (1) is subject to a civil penalty of not more than $10,000.
markdown:
### Chapter 316. State Unif. Traffic Control
##### § 316.1577 — Employer responsibility for violations pertaining to railroad-highway grade crossings

(1) An employer may not knowingly allow, require, permit, or authorize a driver to operate a commercial motor vehicle in violation of a federal, state, or local law or rule pertaining to railroad-highway grade crossings.

(2) A person who violates subsection (1) is subject to a civil penalty of not more than $10,000.

subsections.text: An employer may not knowingly allow, require, permit, or authorize a driver to operate a commercial motor vehicle in violation of a federal, state, or local law or rule pertaining to railroad-highway grade crossings.
A person who violates subsection (1) is subject to a civil penalty of not more than $10,000.
